REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO CARRY ITS BURDEN AND SHOW ITS ENTITLEMENT TO FEES AND COSTS UNDER CCP 998 AND/OR 874.010. LATE-FILED OPPOSITION (WITHOUT LEAVE OF COURT OR EXPLANATION FILED ON THE REPLY DUE DATE) IS STRICKEN. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SUPPORT ITS CLAIM FOR ALL ITS ATTORNEY'S FEES AS INCURRED FOR THE COMMON BENEFIT OF THE PARTIES AND THEREFORE, THE COURT WILL NOT EXERCISE ITS DISCRETION TO APPORTION ANY OF THE DEFENDANT'S FEES TO THE PLAINTIFF. MOVING PARTY FURTHER FAILED TO SHOW THAT THERE WAS A VALID ENFORCEABLE AND REASONABLE CCP 998 OFFER. THE OFFER ATTACHED TO THE MOVING PAPERS IS FOR "FOUR HUNDRED THOUSAND DOLLARS ($475,000)," BUT ACCORDING TO THE DEFENDANT, DEFENDANT WAS WILLING TO ACCEPT THE SUM OF $475,000, NOT THE SUM OF $400,000. FURTHERMORE, MOVING PARTY FAILS TO EXPLAIN HOW PAYING $475,000 IS A BETTER OUTCOME THAN KEEPING 50% INTEREST IN THE PROPERTY WORTH $1,200,000 (PER DEFENDANT'S EVIDENCE). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney Fees
Off Calendar Feb-27-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Gregory P. Wayland; Attorney Real Estate Group, APC; 916-671-3138; appearing as counsel for defendants Robert R. Ruiz and Rosemarie Ruiz.
Peter N. Hadiaris; 415-694-0052; appearing as counsel for plaintiff Sean Angles.

Mr. Wayland contests the court's tentative decision. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES is DENIED. Moving party failed to carry its burden and show its entitlement to fees and costs under CCP 998 and/or 874.010. Late-filed opposition (without leave of Court or explanation filed on the reply due date) is stricken. Moving party failed to support its claim for all its attorney's fees as incurred for the common benefit of the parties and therefore, the court will not exercise its discretion to apportion any of the Defendant's fees to the Plaintiff. Moving party further failed to show that there was a valid enforceable and reasonable CCP 998 offer. The offer attached to the moving papers is for "four hundred thousand dollars ($475,000)," but according to the Defendant, Defendant was willing to accept the sum of $475,000, not the sum of $400,000. Furthermore, moving party fails to explain how paying $475,000 is a better outcome than keeping 50% interest in the property worth $1,200,000 (per Defendant's evidence).

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
